transcript: Hi! How’s everybody doin’ tonight? [delay] Aw, yeah! Los Angeles, hi! I played at La Cita in 2008 live. I don’t know if anybody was here that night…
I was there, David Yao was runnin’ around smackin’ people. Lotta fun—
Alright! So we’re gonna have a little fun tonight. Mike, how are ya doin’? [delay]
So good, so good, so good! Yes—
Awesome! [delay] Excellent, fantastic, wonderful! [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted] Let’s see here. Let’s get into a little stuff, here. I would like to make a phone call, uh—

—here tonight. Oh, sure! And we’re gonna see what goes on with that. If anybody’s got somethin’ to share, why, uh, bring it on. Meanwhile, I’m, uh, dialing a number. It’s ringing right now! And we’re gonna wee what happens. [delay, pitch-shifted] ’Scuse me. We’re gonna see what happens. Have a little indigestion, there, so… [delay, pitch-shifted]
Yeah, I remember playing there. It was a great night. Uh… was a little weird.
But, uhh! You know, whatever floats your boat. Ya know what? Los Angeles, we’re gonna go overseas now—
We’re gonna do something a little different because—
Um, I know they’re gonna answer. And we’re gonna go to Australia, now! Oh, yeah! Yeahyeahyeahyeah!
So it’s ringing. We’ll see what happens. Bear with me, a little bit. ’S a meat market.
[breaks up] …calling Victor Churchill! We will be with you shortly! Please continue to hold—
Thank you for calling Victor Churchill—
...regarded as “The most beautiful butcher shop in the world”. Victor Chur—
Hey, uh, is it possible for me to get any lips for sale tonight?
Oh, yeah, I was lookin’ to get some take-out, like, uh, for tonight—I got a big cookout—lookin’ for some lip [exhales]
Yeah, lip, L-I-P, like five pounds, ten pounds; whatever you can, uh, put together for me up ‘ere. Ya know? [delay, pitch-shifted]
I’m not too sure what “lip” is.
Uh, lip or snout, you know; that region of the animal.
Uh, for tonight. I got a very special recipe tonight, sir. Um—
Uh… It’s—It’s a family recipe. None o’ your concern, really. If you can get me some lip and some snout, that’d be fantastic, okay? For tonight!—
Uh, I’d not be able to get it in.
Where do I have to go for that, anyway?
Uh, not too sure. Most places don’t get that in, so it’s very difficult to get the lips [unclear]—
Correct me if I’m wrong, but isn’t this “The most beautiful meat shop in the entire world”? That’s what I just got through hearing—
It definitely is. It definitely is—
Okay. Well, then, where’s my lip? Where—where’s my lips, ya know?
[chuckles] We don’t reall— We don’t stock it in the store at all times, I’m afraid—
Snout. Well, uh, here. Uh, can—?
Can you talk to the chef a little bit, please?
I’m gonna put the chef on; he can tell you what I need. Alright?
Yeah, so where—where are we at with that, uh…
…uh, those li—that snout, there, guy, or mouth? [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted, sped-up]
Not—not— We haven’t got any, I’m afraid.
Why not?! [pitch-shifted] Why not? Whadda ya need, you want some money or what?
[laughs] It was worth a shot, right?—
Longmont, you’ve got a huge crowd of adoring fans, here, so enjoy yourself—
If you got anything you wanna share, I’m all ears. All ears! I’ve got a buncha folks here I’m gonna try to ring up. Uh—
I just texted you some earlier; maybe there’s something on there that [mimics pitch-shifting] could work.
Yeah, I’m goin’ through those. It’s— I got another one ringing right now. So far, they’ve been, uh, unresponsive. I’m gonna get out my saxophone, here, in a little bit, here—
I can’t hear anything up there, guy. Ya there or what? Work with me, guy—
Oh, are you guys open up ‘ere?
Yeah, I’m lookin to track tonight.
Yeah, it’s just me and a drum set.
I’m just lookin’ to lay down some, uh, some hot tracks in the midnight hour?
Oh, you wanna track a—a session.
Oh, big time! Big time, oh, yeah! Uh-huh! [delay]
Okay. Lemme—lemme transfer you over to our studio manager. Can I get your name and who you’re looking for?
Uh, my name is, uh, Jebediah.
Do you think I got—? Do you think I got a good sound? I just tuned my drums—
And it’s for a—? It’s for a drum session in about an hour, you said?
Yeah, in the midnight hour. Big time. Very important. Oh, yeah, uh-huh! [delay] Uh, so I could be up there in a few minutes, you know. It’s not a big deal, so… [inhales]
Lemme transfer you to my studio manager.
Go ‘head, give it a shot, guy.
So while he’s doin’ that very important thing—Oh!—we got a very special extension for this call. Well, let’s give it a ring of two, here. [exhales] Oh, yeah!
I guess they’re not gonna pick up, so--
…is not available to take your call—
Is that a matter of fact?
Please leave a message after the tone. Press the “Pound” key to end recording. [beep]
Woo! Los Angeles, hello! Okay, so we’re gonna go somewhere else.
The Australian Meat Emporium, is where we’re gonna be headed next. Oh! Big time! [exhales]

And can I get a drink up here, please? Could I get a, uh, vodka and tonic up here, please; that’d be great, thank you, La Cita. And it’s ringing here. Uh, it is ringing. That guy missed out on my drumming—
Hey, guy. Yeah, where are we at with those, uh, with those nostrils, guy? I got a cookout tonight, my friend.
I called a little bit earlier. You’re gonna put ‘em together for a custom order!
Ooh! Let me find out. Can I put you on hold for one second?
I’m at a payphone. I don’t have any more change. [delay] I’m running out of time here—
I think you’ve called the wrong number! This is a butcher shop—
this is Victor Churchill, “The most beautiful butcher shop on earth”—
I know, I’m very familiar with it, sir.
Um… Wha—what was your name, sir?
Oh, big time. [delay] I’m, uh, I’m Jebediah. Who am I speaking with?
Hugo. So what’s it gonna take for some nostril and some lip? [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted]
Don— We don’t have any in at the moment. We can get some in for ya, though.
Oh, yeah! [pitch-shifted] Sounds good. W— How long am I gonna have ta wait up ‘ere? [exhales] [delay, pitch-shifted]
Yeah, where’re we at with those nostrils, guy? Tonight?
H— Where are we at with those nostrils and the lip—on my custom order? [inhales]

Uh, you know, pork or goat or, uh, ram. I’m thinkin’ goat or ram. The c— The recipe did not specify but it comes from my great great grandmother. And, ya know, if it’s good enough for her [laughing] it’s good enough for me, so…! [delay] So yeah, anything that you got’ll be good—from goat or ram or sheep up ‘ere! [exhales]
I don’t think we can get any of that.
I need some for tonight, though, big guy! Whatcha got on hand? You got five, ten pounds on hand?
You got any, uh, lip and maybe some Colby-Jack cheese up ‘ere?!
What—what do you all got up ‘ere? [exhales]
You guys open for business up ‘ere? [delay] What do you got goin’ up ‘ere, anyway? Huh? What time y’all close, anyway?
Listen, guy, if— Okay, if I can just get some urine and some tails, I’ll scrounge the rest up, okay? So just put it together—
Surely you can do that for me, right?
Oh, I’ll be there, not a problem.
Oh, okay! Take care o’ yourself!
Take care, yeah, bye-bye, uh-huh, yeah! [delay]
Doomie’s in Hollywood]. How can I help you?
Yeah, hello! “Is this Doomie’s?” I said, for the fourth time.
You’re comin’ in kinda choppy, though.

Yeah, so are you. You don’t hear me whinin’ about it. But anyway, um, I don’t have a copy o’ your menu. What’s on special tonight up ‘ere, guy? Whatcha got? [delay] You guys got any Colby-Jack up ‘ere? [delay]
Well, we tried! We—we—we tried Doomie’s; they weren’t having it, were they?—
That’s the one I sent you.
That’s, uh, my wife’s ex-stepfather asshole.
Uhh… No, there’s not a “Tony” on here. But there’s a lotta—
I know, just—just go to the fir— The first number I sent you, call it. He won’t answer, but you can give it a fighting shot!—
Oh, okay. I see. That was the one I was about ta dial, coincidentally. So, okay, here we go—
This is the one I’m calling; this is the one! This is the one, this is the one, yeah! Here we go. I’m dialing it. Like you say, he may or may not be responsive at this particular juncture.
But, uh, we’re gonna see here.
Anybody gon’ respond up ‘ere? You got any colby jack up ‘ere?! [delay]—
Your call has been forwarded to an automatic voice message system. 2-1-3-[redacted]–
Your recording has reached its maximum length. Message sent. Goodbye!—
Oh, hell of a deal! Hell of a deal.
At the tone, please record your message.
Nobody there. You guys still with me tonight? Alright!
I got another one on the— I got another one on the pipeline, here, if you bear with me for one sec.
This one is ringing… What were you gonna say up ‘ere?!
I was gonna say, w—we got a— We got a credible number coming from the audience right now—a credible number!—
Okay, please— Please hold that credible number for ten seconds. We’ll see what happens here. Please hold that thought for me for just a sec up ‘ere.
Oh, am I correct? Good. Hey, uh, I need to— I need to come up there with a party o’ six tonight. I need a couple rooms tonight. [exhales]
Very important. We— We’re coming from Tokyo tonight. We just flew in for 34 hours in the air! We gotta get it there and we gotta get there, pronto, okay, guy? [delay] So what can you do for us?
What can you do tonight? [delay, pitch-shifted]—
Each person, one bed, two bed?
Uh, whatever ya got; three-bed, two-bed, one-bed, you know, it’s fine. [delay] Uh-huh! [delay] So we’re ready, uh—
So we— Can we show up in about ten minutes, then? Is it alright?
I need your credit card and your ID, yes.
Okay, lemme just give you my credit card number now and you get things running. And, uh, you guys got any, uh, triangular beds up ‘ere?
Or an AVCARD. Do you have AVCARDs at all? For the guests.
Okay, here, talk to my guest. He’s gonna pay you. Here, one sec. Talk to my guest. He’s gonna give you the credit card, okay?
Okay, here ya go. I’m just gonna hand—hand the phone over. Sure, not a problem. Just a second.
Thank you for calling Doomie’s. A server will be with you in one second.
You guys got any vacancies up ‘ere?
As in…? For our storefront or…
…for parking spots? For storefronts, I do know—
I know that there’s one open right now, but y— This is the wrong number to call—
Maybe you’re the wrong person to talk to. But that’s okay. I’m not gonna hold it against ya. Hey, can you do me a big favor?
Can you co—meet me in the hot tub? Just for a few minutes. Go over thin— I would like to go over a few things with ya.
Hey! Come on, come back! [delay] Los Angeles, woo! [delay, pitch-shifted]
Okay, I’m ready for your numerals. I sure am. If you’ve still got those available—
[quietly] Yes, I—I really hope he answers this time—
Hi. Whadda ya got goin’ tonight?
—the first one, who watches a lotta porn.
If anyone’s got another exchange we can try… uh, please prepare that.
Yes! We—we’re—we’re accepting numbers now for people who will answer the phone. Oh, we got one! We got one coming. We got one coming.
And then you’re off the hook, because—because, my god, you’ve done your duty!
I need some udders. Um, can you help me out with that?
Udders, uh, and nostrils, uh, for my—
Yeah, we don’t have any, buddy. Any—anything else, mate?
Well, I— Where can I get somethin’ like that? Can you refer me? I got a special recipe I c— need to cook tonight.
Any urine or any testes, anything like that?
That butcher is, uh, apparently very occupied right now. Okay, those numerals, sir, kind sir?
[to audience] ’Kay, he just wants the numerals. [to LPC ] 2-5-5-[redacted]: no context.
Oh, no context! Okay, even better. Okay, here we go. I’m just going in cold to this one and we’ll see what happens up ‘ere. Maybe they got some, uh… [delay] Maybe they got some Colby-Jack up ‘ere—
Okay. Hey, there’s another number. Who is there? Somebody in the audience… is, uh… faxing me, like three pages in a row, right now.
Yeah, I was lookin’ for some, um, some schnitzel up ‘ere.
No, we don’t sell the schnitzel here.
Well this is a German b— What kinda German butcher is it that doesn’t have schnitzel?
We’re a German butcher but we sell the deli and cafe.
What—what do you idiots have up there? Just outta curiosity
…you wanna [unclear] otherwise you can answer. [to LPC ] You probably ring the wrong place, because next to us is a German butchery; they sell the raw meat. But we don’t sell the raw meat.
Maybe you should go, uh, work over there, is what I’m kinda hearing. You know what I mean? Have you considered that, or…?
Sorry, if you ring and just ask this question, please call them.
Okay, is it possible for me to get any urination from you at all? Or maybe some regurgitation at all?
I’m not interested what you’re asking. Thank you for calling—
You’re “not interested” in what I’m asking?
Whadda you sell up there? [laughs] Okay, Los Angeles, thank you! [delay]
Los Angeles, you’re the best! The absolute best in the world— [delay]
in the world, in the world, in the world! [delay]
